result.name: Literature Minor
result.templateAssets:
[{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

UC Santa Cruz students may complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and credit limits. Minors are recorded on the official transcript but do not appear on the diploma. Pursuing a minor is optional and should align with a student’s academic interests and career goals.


Students do not apply for minors as part of their UC Santa Cruz admission application. Transfer students interested in a minor should reach out to the department that offers it for advising during their first quarter of enrollment.

INFORMATION FOR ALL MINORS","groupId":"c3756f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"Literature Minor","groupId":"c4756f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Students majoring in a different program of study may find that a literature minor is an invaluable addition to their educational plan. Prospective transfer students interested in this minor are encouraged to complete the articulated equivalents of TWO lower-division courses prior to transfer. Refer to the “Preparation for the Minor” section below for courses approved to articulate to these requirements.


Please visit the Literature Department website to learn more about this minor.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Literature Minor Program Statement
